help_text.antechamber:
[31mUsage: antechamber -i    [0m input file name
[31m                   -fi   [0m input file format
[31m                   -o    [0m output file name
[31m                   -fo   [0m output file format
[31m                   -c    [0m charge method
[31m                   -cf   [0m charge file name
[31m                   -nc   [0m net molecular charge (int)
[31m                   -a    [0m additional file name
[31m                   -fa   [0m additional file format
[31m                   -ao   [0m additional file operation
[34m                          crd   [0m: only read in coordinate
[34m                          crg   [0m: only read in charge
[34m                          radius[0m: only read in radius
[34m                          name  [0m: only read in atom name
[34m                          type  [0m: only read in atom type
[34m                          bond  [0m: only read in bond type 
[31m                   -m    [0m multiplicity (2S+1), default is 1
[31m                   -rn   [0m residue name, overrides input file, default is MOL
[31m                   -rf   [0m residue toplogy file name in prep input file,
                          default is molecule.res
[31m                   -ch   [0m check file name for gaussian, default is 'molecule'
[31m                   -ek   [0m mopac or sqm keyword, inside quotes; overwrites previous ones
[31m                   -gk   [0m gaussian job keyword, inside quotes, is ignored when both -gopt and -gsp are used
[31m                   -gopt [0m gaussian job keyword for optimization, inside quotes
[31m                   -gsp  [0m gaussian job keyword for single point calculation, inside quotes
[31m                   -gm   [0m gaussian memory keyword, inside quotes, such as "%mem=1000MB"
[31m                   -gn   [0m gaussian number of processors keyword, inside quotes, such as "%nproc=8"
[31m                   -gdsk [0m gaussian maximum disk usage keyword, inside quotes, such as "%maxdisk=50GB"
[31m                   -gv   [0m add keyword to generate gesp file (for Gaussian 09 only)
[34m                          1    [0m: yes
[34m                          0    [0m: no, the default
[31m                   -ge   [0m gaussian esp file generated by iop(6/50=1), default is g09.gesp
[31m                   -tor  [0m torsional angle list, inside a pair of quotes, such as "1-2-3-4:0,5-6-7-8"
[34m                         [0m ':1' or ':0' indicates the torsional angle is frozen or not
[31m                   -df   [0m am1-bcc precharge flag, 2 - use sqm(default); 0 - use mopac
[31m                   -at   [0m atom type
[34m                          gaff [0m: the default
[34m                          gaff2[0m: for gaff2 (beta-version)
[34m                          amber[0m: for PARM94/99/99SB
[34m                          bcc  [0m: bcc 
[34m                          abcg2[0m: abcg2 
[34m                          sybyl[0m: sybyl 
[31m                   -du   [0m fix duplicate atom names: yes(y)[default] or no(n)
[31m                   -bk   [0m component/block Id, for ccif
[31m                   -an   [0m adjust atom names: yes(y) or no(n)
                          the default is 'y' for 'mol2' and 'ac' and 'n' for the other formats 
[31m                   -j    [0m atom type and bond type prediction index, default is 4 
[34m                          0    [0m: no assignment
[34m                          1    [0m: atom type 
[34m                          2    [0m: full  bond types 
[34m                          3    [0m: part  bond types 
[34m                          4    [0m: atom and full bond type 
[34m                          5    [0m: atom and part bond type 
[31m                   -s    [0m status information: 0(brief), 1(default) or 2(verbose)
[31m                   -eq   [0m equalizing atomic charge, default is 1 for '-c resp', '-c bcc', '-c abcg2' and 0 for the other charge metho
help_text.parmchk2:
[31mUsage: parmchk2 -i   [0m input file name
[31m                -o   [0m frcmod file name
[31m                -f   [0m input file format (prepi, prepc, ac, mol2, frcmod, leaplog) 
[31m                -s   [0m ff parm set, it is suppressed by "-p" option
[34m                      1 or gaff:   [0m gaff (the default)
[34m                      2 or gaff2:  [0m gaff2 
[34m                      3 or parm99: [0m parm99
[34m                      4 or parm10: [0m parm10
[34m                      5 or lipid14:[0m lipid14
[31m                -frc [0m frcmod files to be loaded, the supported frcmods include
[31m                     [0m ff99SB, ff14SB, ff03 for proteins , bsc1, ol15, ol3 for DNA and yil for RNA
[31m                     [0m eg. ff14SB+bsc1+yil, ff99SB+bsc1
[31m                -p   [0m parmfile, suppress '-s' flag, optional
[31m                -pf  [0m parmfile format 
[34m                      1:[0m for amber FF data file (the default)
[34m                      2:[0m for additional force field parameter file
[31m                -afrc[0m additional frcmod file, no matter using -p or not, optional
[31m                -c   [0m atom type corresponding score file, default is PARMCHK.DAT
[31m                -atc [0m additional atom type corresponding score file, optional
[31m                     [0m type 'parmchk2 -l' to learn details
[31m                -a   [0m print out all force field parameters including those in the parmfile
[31m                     [0m can be 'Y' (yes) or 'N' (no) default is 'N' 
[31m                -w   [0m print out parameters that matching improper dihedral parameters
[31m                     [0m that contain 'X' in the force field parameter file, can be 'Y' (yes)
[31m                     [0m or 'N' (no), default is 'Y'
[31m                -fc  [0m option of force constant calculation for '-f frcmod' or '-f leaplog'
[34m                      1:[0m default behavior (the default option)
[34m                      2:[0m do empirical calculation before using corresponding atom types
[31m                -att [0m for the frcmod input format, option of performing parmchk
[34m                      1:[0m for all parameters (the default)
[34m                      2:[0m only for those with ATTN
help_text.tleap:
-I: Adding /root/.local/share/mamba/envs/AmberTools26/dat/leap/prep to search path.
-I: Adding /root/.local/share/mamba/envs/AmberTools26/dat/leap/lib to search path.
-I: Adding /root/.local/share/mamba/envs/AmberTools26/dat/leap/parm to search path.
-I: Adding /root/.local/share/mamba/envs/AmberTools26/dat/leap/cmd to search path.
Usage: /root/.local/share/mamba/envs/AmberTools26/bin/teLeap [options]
Options:
 -h         Generate this message.
 -s         Ignore leaprc startup file.
 -I {dir}   Add {dir} to search path.
 -f {file}  Source {file}.

commands.command: antechamber -i zqz_input.sdf -fi sdf -o zqz_gaff2_am1bcc.mol2 -fo mol2 -at gaff2 -c bcc -nc 0 -rn ZQZ -s 2

warnings: ZQZ parameters are ligand-only inputs for future setup; they are not MD results.
Future 8GLA setup manifest must link PARAMETER_AUDIT.md and all parameter file hashes before minimization.
remaining_launch_blockers: Official package still records do_not_run_md: true; execution remains on hold.
Future explicit Phase 5 launch authorization record is absent.
